raw_output_payload.reasoning:
Need command: find /testbed -name '*.php' -type f -printf '%p\n' | sort | while read f; do echo -n "$f: "; wc -l < "$f"; done

Return one command. Use xargs? Use awk? Provide single pipeline. Let's output: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | sort -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do echo -n "$f: "; wc -l < "$f"; done

But sort -z requires GNU sort. Simpler: find ... | sort | while read f; do echo -n "$f: "; wc -l < "$f"; done

Return that.
